rulings.Case Title: HASSAN NAJAFI ET AL VS. MAJOR CHABOT PARTNERS, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2023 line 1. HEARING RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION Hearing Re: Preliminary Injunction is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry its burden to support the requested relief. The Court finds no basis to enjoin or vacate the arbitration award as sought in this motion for preliminary injunction. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TINA JAZINSKI ET AL VS. AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC., AIRBNB, INC. Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is GRANTED. Parties are bound by the terms of the arbitration agreements and exceptions thereto. There is no dispute as to the existence of the agreements and the language of the relevant provisions, e.g. Terms of Service, sections 23.4 and 23.5. A claim for public injunctive relief along with claims for infringement, emergency injunctive relief, individual claim of sexual assault or harassment is expressly excepted from the arbitration clause and are to be brought in a judicial proceeding in a court of competent jurisdiction. Plaintiff filed this action purportedly asserting a public injunctive relief claim. Defendant appears to argue that before Plaintiff may bring such a claim in Court, Plaintiff must first obtain a ruling from an arbitrator that the claim in fact is one for public injunctive relief: "Taken together, these Sections clearly and unmistakably require Plaintiff to file his claims with the AAA, wherein an arbitrator will decide the arbitrability of Plaintiff's claims, including his or her power to hear those claims under the TOS, the AAA Rules, and the Federal Arbitration Act." (Reply 4:3-6). In other words, defendant appears to argue that before the parties may avail themselves of a judicial proceeding, they need to seek a determination from the arbitrator if their proposed claim in fact is an, for example, "emergency" with "exigent circumstances," or that claim for sexual assault is sufficiently stated, or all elements of copy right infringement claim are asserted and can be proven, or as in this case, a proper claim for public injunction is stated. Defendant cites no authority for this proposition that the claims expressly excepted from the arbitration clause (including emergency relief) first need to be approved by the arbitrator as claims not subjected to the arbitration clause. Therefore, it is up to this Court to determine whether the claim, as presented to this Court, in the First Amended Complaint falls within the exception to the arbitration clause. Reading the operative complaint as a whole, including specifically paragraphs 138, 139, 145 and 149 the Court finds that this action seeks to redress or prevent injury to an individual plaintiff- or to a group of individuals similarly situated to the plaintiff, that is not an appropriate claim for a public injunctive relief which "by and large benefit[] the general public and  the plaintiff, if at all, only incidentally and/or as a member of the general public." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMIE EDSON ET AL VS. AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Airbnb, Inc. And Airbnb Payments, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT AIRBNB PAYMENTS, INC., AIRBNB, INC. Defendants Airbnb, Inc. And Airbnb Payments, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is GRANTED. Parties are bound by the terms of the arbitration agreements and exceptions thereto. There is no dispute as to the existence of the agreements and the language of the relevant provisions, e.g. Terms of Service, sections 23.4 and 23.5. A claim for public injunctive relief along with claims for infringement, emergency injunctive relief, individual claim of sexual assault or harassment is expressly excepted from the arbitration clause and are to be brought in a judicial proceeding in a court of competent jurisdiction. Plaintiff filed this action purportedly asserting a public injunctive relief claim. Defendant appears to argue that before Plaintiff may bring such a claim in Court, Plaintiff must first obtain a ruling from an arbitrator that the claim in fact is one for public injunctive relief: "Taken together, these Sections clearly and unmistakably require Plaintiff to file his claims with the AAA, wherein an arbitrator will decide the arbitrability of Plaintiff's claims, including his or her power to hear those claims under the TOS, the AAA Rules, and the Federal Arbitration Act." (Reply 4:3-6). In other words, defendant appears to argue that before the parties may avail themselves of a judicial proceeding, they need to seek a determination from the arbitrator if their proposed claim in fact is an, for example, "emergency" with "exigent circumstances," or that claim for sexual assault is sufficiently stated, or all elements of copy right infringement claim are asserted and can be proven, or as in this case, a proper claim for public injunction is stated. Defendant cites no authority for this proposition that the claims expressly excepted from the arbitration clause (including emergency relief) first need to be approved by the arbitrator as claims not subjected to the arbitration clause. Therefore, it is up to this Court to determine whether the claim, as presented to this Court, in the First Amended Complaint falls within the exception to the arbitration clause. Reading the operative complaint as a whole, including specifically paragraphs 92, 93, 99 and 105 the Court finds that this action seeks to redress or prevent injury to an individual plaintiff- or to a group of individuals similarly situated to the plaintiff, that is not an appropriate claim for a public injunctive relief which "by and large benefit[] the general public and  the plaintiff, if at all, only incidentally and/or as a member of the general public." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
